extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo won from the early break, while ALAPHILIPPE Julian defended yellow on the roughest alpine stage despite GC riders' late attacks.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA joined early break exploiting Movistar's willingness to chase their own rider, nearly threatening GC before team intervention.
BERNAL Egan attacked closing kilometers to gain 27 seconds on THOMAS Geraint, moving above teammate despite insufficient gap for podium.
BENNETT George crashed at 6km indicating technical descending sections more dangerous than dry finishing streets despite late rain.
BARGUIL Warren sprinted for KOM points throughout stage to reclaim polka dots from leader, showing two-week competition window exploitable.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Fourth career Tour stage win demonstrates renewed climbing form after acceleration dropped BARDET and entire break on Galibier.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Survived roughest alpine day without teammates, two climbs from immortality as first French winner since 1985.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: Tactical attack in finale gained time on THOMAS despite insufficient margin, signals team leadership shift underway.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: Second place and KOM lead masks disappointment at inability to match QUINTANA's acceleration, indicates limits reached.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Lost 27 seconds and GC place to teammate BERNAL on decisive alpine stage, captaincy clearly shifting.
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: Second crash of Tour at high-speed 6km turn required medical attention, pattern of ill fortune continuing.
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: negative: Mechanical requiring long bike change wait cost finishing position, team support gap exposed on critical stage.
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: neutral: Missed QUINTANA acceleration but finished with break remnants, confirming tier below elite climbers on Galibier.
